mechanism_narrative: A persistently high federal funds rate above 4.5% compresses equity valuations through elevated discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward 20-day returns as long as the rate remains above that threshold.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed rate at 5.25% as of Aug 2006, near peak of tightening cycle
failure_condition: Over a 2-year rolling window, the rank IC of FEDFUNDS level with 20-day forward returns is not negative and below -0.03, or the mean forward return during periods with FEDFUNDS > 4.5% is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.0% from a low base, it signals imminent labor market weakness that reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward 60-day returns.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.7% as of Aug 2006, historically low
failure_condition: In periods following UNRATE crossing above 5.0, the average 60-day forward return is not negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) or the win rate is not below 40% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Combined decline in housing starts (HOUST) and a high federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates tightening monetary policy is cooling residential investment, which depresses GDP growth and earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts peaked in early 2006 and were declining by July 2006
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with housing downturn
failure_condition: Over a 3-year rolling window, the interaction term (HOUST * FEDFUNDS) in a linear model has insignificant coefficient (p > 0.05) or the direction of the combined signal does not consistently predict negative forward returns (IC < -0.02).

mechanism_narrative: In a low unemployment regime (UNRATE below 5.0%), positive payroll growth (PAYEMS) signals a tight labor market that boosts consumer income and spending, leading to positive S&P 500 forward 20-day returns. This mechanism is only active when unemployment is already low.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.7% and PAYEMS increasing as of Aug 2006
failure_condition: In periods where UNRATE < 5.0% and PAYEMS increases month-over-month, the average 20-day forward return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the win rate is below 55% over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A strong increase in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust consumer demand that drives corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward 5-day returns as markets quickly reprice earnings expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS August 2006 value was 368,233 million, revised to 353,285
failure_condition: Over a 3-year rolling window, the rank IC of RSAFS month-over-month change with 5-day forward returns is below 0.03 or the directional accuracy is less than 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) exceeding 0.5% indicates a significant slowdown in manufacturing activity that precedes broader economic contraction, producing negative S&P 500 forward 60-day returns.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO August 2006 was 113.71 (vintage), later revised to 99.48
failure_condition: In the 60 trading days following an INDPRO monthly decline > 0.5%, the average forward return is not negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) or the probability of a negative return is less than 60% over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in both industrial production (INDPRO) and nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals synchronized expansion in supply and labor markets, reinforcing each other to boost corporate output and employment, producing positive S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Both series were positive in mid-2006 expansion
failure_condition: Over a 3-year rolling window, the interaction effect (INDPRO change * PAYEMS change) has a non-positive coefficient in a linear regression predicting 20-day returns, or the combined signal's directional accuracy is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains below 0.2% month-over-month (stable inflation), the Federal Reserve is unlikely to raise interest rates further, removing a key source of equity risk. This stable inflation regime produces positive S&P 500 forward 20-day returns as rate hike fears subside.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL August 2006 was 203.7, monthly change from prior month was 0.3% (revised 0.2%)
failure_condition: In months where CPIAUCSL change is below 0.2%, the average 20-day forward return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the win rate is less than 55% over any 2-year window.
